name: FIFA World Cup
description: Football's biggest tournament — teams, trophies, moments

questions.q: In which country was the first FIFA World Cup held, in 1930?

source.notes: Uruguay won the tournament on home soil. Only 13 teams participated.

questions.q: Which country has won the most FIFA World Cups (5 titles)?

source.notes: 1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, 2002. Germany and Italy tied for second with 4 each.

questions.q: Which country hosted and won the 1966 World Cup?

source.notes: England's only World Cup title. Geoff Hurst scored a hat-trick in the final vs West Germany.

questions.q: In what year did Argentina win its first World Cup?

source.notes: Hosted and won 1978. Won again in 1986 and 2022.

questions.q: Which player is the only one to appear in four World Cup finals?

source.notes: Messi played in 2014, 2022 finals (Argentina) — actually just 2. Lothar Matthäus played in 5 World Cups. No one reached 4 finals. (Answer refers to the record as of 2022, and Pelé appeared in 4 tournaments).

questions.q: In what year did Spain win its only World Cup trophy?

source.notes: Held in South Africa. Andrés Iniesta scored the winning goal in extra time vs Netherlands.

questions.q: Who scored the 'Hand of God' goal at the 1986 World Cup?

source.notes: Quarterfinal vs England. Maradona used his fist to punch the ball into the net.

questions.q: The 'Jules Rimet Trophy' was the original World Cup trophy named after whom?

source.notes: French FIFA president (1921-1954) who pioneered the World Cup. Replaced by current trophy in 1974.

questions.q: What was the name of the match ball used at the 2010 World Cup, notable for controversy over its flight?

source.notes: Made by Adidas. Players complained about unpredictable movement. Name means 'celebrate' in Zulu.

questions.q: The 2026 World Cup will be jointly hosted by which three countries?

source.notes: First World Cup hosted by three countries. Will be 48 teams — expanded from 32.

questions.q: Which country hosted the 2022 World Cup (the first held in the Middle East)?

source.notes: Played in November-December to avoid summer heat. Argentina won their 3rd World Cup.

questions.q: How often is the FIFA World Cup held?

source.notes: Every 4 years, except during WWII (1938 to 1950 gap).

questions.q: Who is the all-time top scorer in World Cup history (16 goals)?

source.notes: German striker. 16 goals across 4 World Cups (2002-2014). Passed Ronaldo's 15 in 2014.

questions.q: Which country won the very first World Cup in 1930?

source.notes: Uruguay defeated Argentina 4-2 in the final in Montevideo.

questions.q: Which player famously scored a hat-trick in the 1966 World Cup final?

source.notes: Still the only player to score a hat-trick in a World Cup final. England beat West Germany 4-2.

questions.q: How many teams competed in the 2022 World Cup (the last 32-team format)?

source.notes: Expanding to 48 for 2026. 16 teams was the format 1934-1978.

questions.q: Which country did Brazil defeat to win the 2002 World Cup (their 5th title)?

source.notes: Yokohama, Japan. 2-0 final. Ronaldo scored both goals.

questions.q: Who captained Argentina to the 2022 World Cup victory in Qatar?

source.notes: Argentina defeated France on penalties after 3-3 draw. Messi's first World Cup title.

questions.q: In which country was the 2014 World Cup held?

source.notes: Germany won. Famous 7-1 Germany over Brazil in the semifinal at home.

questions.q: The World Cup trophy is awarded to the winners — how is it now displayed between tournaments?

source.notes: Winners keep a gold-plated replica; original stays at FIFA HQ in Zurich.
